title: AI-901 Mock Exam 3: Workloads and Implementation
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AI-901 skills measured from April 15, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

domains.title: Implement AI solutions by using Microsoft Foundry

items.prompt: An image-captioning service consistently produces lower-quality descriptions for assistive-technology users. Which principle should guide remediation?
items.explanation: Inclusiveness considers whether the system works for people with different needs and abilities. The issue is user access and quality, not grouping, cost, or language conversion.

items.prompt: A team cannot explain who approved a model change that caused harmful outcomes. Which governance gap is most evident?
items.explanation: Accountability requires defined owners, approval records, and responsibility for outcomes. Detection, tokenization, and elasticity do not address missing governance.

items.prompt: {{blank}} is evaluated by testing whether error rates differ across affected groups.
items.explanation: Fairness evaluation looks for systematic differences in performance or outcomes across relevant groups. The alternatives describe model size, media generation, or capacity.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about privacy-aware AI design.

statements.text: Collect only data that is necessary for the stated purpose
Store every prompt forever in case it becomes useful
Make model interaction logs public to simplify troubleshooting

items.explanation: Data minimization and controlled access reduce privacy and security risk. Indefinite retention and public troubleshooting logs expand exposure without a defined need.

items.prompt: Which TWO actions support reliability and safety after deployment?
answers: Monitor failures and changes in production behavior
Define an escalation or fallback process
options: Monitor failures and changes in production behavior
Define an escalation or fallback process
Remove human intervention for every high-impact case
items.explanation: Monitoring detects changed behavior and escalation limits harm when the system is uncertain or failing. Permanent assumptions and eliminating oversight weaken safety.

items.prompt: Which TWO pieces of information improve transparency for users?
answers: The intended purpose and important limitations
Notice that AI is contributing to the interaction
options: The intended purpose and important limitations
Notice that AI is contributing to the interaction
Private credentials used by the backend
Proprietary model weights regardless of user need
items.explanation: Useful transparency explains AI involvement, purpose, and limitations without exposing secrets or irrelevant proprietary artifacts.

items.prompt: A search system compares the semantic meaning of short passages. Which model output is most useful?
items.explanation: Embeddings represent semantic characteristics numerically and support similarity comparison. Audio, visual coordinates, and cost forecasts are unrelated outputs.

items.prompt: A creative-writing assistant returns nearly identical drafts. Which setting could be raised cautiously to increase variation?
items.explanation: Raising temperature can increase sampling variety, although outputs still require evaluation. Context length, permissions, and index count are not creativity controls.

items.prompt: A smaller model meets quality and latency requirements in testing. Why might it be preferable to a larger model?
items.answer: It can reduce cost and latency while preserving required quality
options: It can reduce cost and latency while preserving required quality
It always has more current knowledge
It removes the need for monitoring
items.explanation: A model that meets measured quality with lower operational cost or latency can be the better fit. Size does not guarantee freshness, safety, or freedom from monitoring.

items.prompt: Supplying retrieved company documents with each request is called {{blank}} the model.
items.explanation: Grounding provides relevant external context for a response without changing model weights. Clustering, segmentation, and synthesis refer to other operations.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about model context and output.

statements.text: Input and output tokens contribute to model usage
Maximum output tokens guarantees factual accuracy
A deployment should be tested under expected concurrency

items.explanation: Token limits control capacity and usage but cannot guarantee correctness. Load testing is needed to understand latency and throughput under realistic concurrency.

items.prompt: Which TWO factors should be compared before changing a production model deployment?
answers: Latency and cost under expected use
options: Latency and cost under expected use
The alphabetical order of model names
The color used for the deployment icon
items.explanation: A production selection should use measured task quality and operational performance. Display order and icon styling provide no evidence of model suitability.

items.prompt: A system predicts the expected wait time in minutes. Which task type is this?
items.explanation: A numeric duration is a continuous value suited to regression. Classification returns labels, clustering finds groups, and OCR reads visual text.

items.prompt: A model identifies whether an email expresses satisfaction, frustration, or neutrality. Which capability is being used?
items.explanation: Sentiment analysis estimates expressed opinion in text. Entity recognition finds names, object detection locates visual items, and language detection identifies the language used.

items.prompt: A camera system must read the characters on vehicle plates. Which capability is required?
items.explanation: OCR extracts visible characters from images. Generation creates new images, summarization condenses language, and clustering groups data.

items.prompt: A model identifies mentions of dates, people, and companies in reports. Which text technique fits?
items.explanation: Named entity recognition locates and categorizes specific mentions such as people, organizations, and dates. The other tasks do not produce those entity labels.

items.prompt: An autonomous support workflow chooses whether to search documentation, query an order system, or escalate. Which workload pattern is this?
items.explanation: An agentic workflow selects tools and actions toward a support goal. The alternatives analyze images, audio, or unlabeled data without action orchestration.

items.prompt: A service produces concise bullet points from a lengthy incident report. Which technique is required?
items.explanation: Summarization creates a shorter representation of source content. Entity linking, visual detection, and transcription produce different outputs.

items.prompt: {{blank}} discovers natural customer segments when no segment labels exist.
items.explanation: Clustering groups similar unlabeled observations. Classification needs target labels, anomaly detection finds unusual cases, and captioning describes visual content.

items.prompt: Decide whether each stated output matches the technique.

statements.text: Regression can predict a numeric energy demand
Image classification returns a bounding box for every object
Key phrase extraction returns a complete spoken narration

items.explanation: Regression returns numbers. Per-object locations come from object detection rather than image classification, and speech synthesis—not key phrase extraction—creates narration.

items.prompt: Which TWO inputs can a multimodal model use together for visual question answering?
answers: A text question about the image
options: A text question about the image
An Azure subscription invoice only as billing metadata
A speech voice name with no audio or image content
items.explanation: Visual question answering combines image content with language instructions. Billing metadata and a voice name do not provide the visual evidence or question.

items.prompt: Which TWO scenarios are information-extraction workloads?
answers: Return named fields from a scanned form
Return structured events and timestamps from a video
options: Return named fields from a scanned form
Return structured events and timestamps from a video
items.explanation: Extraction derives grounded structure from supplied content. Creating an image and synthesizing speech generate new media instead.

items.prompt: A developer needs a quick no-code way to configure agent instructions and tools in Foundry. Which agent type should be selected?
options: Hosted agent with a custom container
items.explanation: Prompt agents are configured through instructions, models, and tools without a custom orchestration runtime. Hosted agents and the other resources serve different needs.

items.prompt: A team already runs custom agent code and wants to call Foundry models through a common API. Which API family is designed for model responses and tools?
items.explanation: The Responses API provides a model interaction surface that can support tools and response state. DNS, cost, and storage APIs do not perform generative inference.

items.prompt: An agent can delete customer records. Which authorization design is safest?
items.answer: Expose a narrowly scoped operation and require verified user permission
options: Expose a narrowly scoped operation and require verified user permission
Give the agent a subscription Owner credential
Use one shared administrator key for all users
Authorize deletion whenever the model sounds confident
items.explanation: A high-impact tool should expose only the required action and enforce application identity and user authorization. Broad or shared credentials and model confidence are not authorization.

items.prompt: A product assistant must cite uploaded manuals and decline unsupported questions. Which configuration best fits?
items.answer: File search plus instructions to answer only from retrieved evidence
options: File search plus instructions to answer only from retrieved evidence
Image generation with a product logo
items.explanation: File search supplies relevant manual passages, while explicit instructions define behavior when evidence is missing. Sampling or unrelated tools cannot provide grounded product facts.

items.prompt: A team needs to compare prompt variants over a fixed evaluation set. What should it record?
items.answer: Task-quality metrics and the tested prompt or deployment version
options: Task-quality metrics and the tested prompt or deployment version
The browser zoom used by the tester
A random answer with no input record
items.explanation: Repeatable evaluation records the version, inputs, and relevant quality measures. Appearance or untracked random examples cannot support a controlled comparison.

items.prompt: Why should an application validate structured model output before using it in a database update?
items.answer: Model output can be malformed or contain unsupported values
options: Model output can be malformed or contain unsupported values
Validation increases the model context window
items.explanation: Generated output is untrusted application input and must satisfy the expected schema and business rules. Validation does not alter context, authorization, or knowledge grounding.

items.prompt: A deployed agent uses file {{blank}} to retrieve uploaded documents.
items.explanation: File search retrieves relevant content from files for grounded responses. The other terms describe media or classification operations.

items.prompt: Use {{blank}} records to inspect model and tool execution steps.
items.explanation: Traces capture the path through model, retrieval, and tool operations for diagnosis. Embeddings represent content, invoices are documents, and subnets are network boundaries.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about prompt design.

statements.text: A system prompt can define enduring behavior and boundaries
A user prompt should automatically override every safety instruction
Examples can clarify the required response format

items.explanation: System instructions establish higher-priority behavior, and examples can make output expectations concrete. User input does not automatically supersede system or safety controls.

items.prompt: Which TWO practices support a secure lightweight Foundry client?
answers: Use a managed identity or other supported credential
Keep privileged credentials out of public client code
options: Use a managed identity or other supported credential
Keep privileged credentials out of public client code
Place an administrator key in the system prompt
Allow anonymous access to every project tool
items.explanation: Supported identity and server-side secret handling reduce credential exposure. Prompt-embedded administrator keys and anonymous tools create excessive risk.

items.prompt: Which text capability can find order numbers and product names in a message?
items.explanation: Named entity recognition finds and categorizes specified mentions in text. Sentiment estimates opinion, segmentation labels image pixels, and regression predicts numbers.

items.prompt: A service must translate live spoken Spanish into English text. Which capability should it use?
items.explanation: Speech translation recognizes spoken input and translates it to a target language. Synthesis and the other analytical tasks do not perform that end-to-end conversion.

items.prompt: A transcription system often misses product codes. What is an appropriate first adaptation?
items.answer: Supply a phrase list containing expected product codes
options: Supply a phrase list containing expected product codes
items.explanation: A phrase list supplies contextual vocabulary that can improve recognition of expected terms. Visual, governance, and image-generation changes do not affect transcription.

items.prompt: Separating speakers in a meeting transcript is called speaker {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Speaker diarization identifies which speaker produced each segment of audio. The other terms do not specifically assign transcript segments to speakers.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about text analysis.

statements.text: Summarization can condense a long conversation
Key phrase extraction creates a complete audio narration
Sentiment analysis returns the location of every object in a photo

items.explanation: Summarization condenses language. Key phrase extraction returns text fragments rather than audio, and sentiment analysis estimates opinion rather than locating visual objects.

items.prompt: Which TWO components are needed for a conventional voice assistant pipeline?
answers: Speech synthesis after a text response is produced
options: Speech synthesis after a text response is produced
items.explanation: A conventional voice pipeline transcribes spoken input and synthesizes the resulting text response. Visual segmentation and detection are unrelated.

items.prompt: A moderation workflow needs to label an uploaded image with one of several known content categories. Which task fits?
items.explanation: Image classification selects a known category for the overall image. Visual question answering responds about image content, OCR reads visible text, and regression predicts numbers.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A maintenance app needs the coordinates of each corroded area in a photograph. Which output should the model produce?
items.explanation: Locations of multiple visual defects require detection regions or boxes. Sentiment, transcripts, and customer clustering do not represent image locations.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A model is asked to compare two diagrams supplied with a text instruction. Which capability is essential?
items.answer: A deployment that accepts visual inputs
options: A deployment that accepts visual inputs
items.explanation: The task requires a deployment capable of interpreting images with text. Text-only embeddings, sentiment analysis, and audio transcription cannot inspect diagrams.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Finding the location of a bicycle in an image uses object {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Object detection identifies and locates visual instances. Synthesis, translation, and summarization do not return object positions.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about image generation and analysis.

statements.text: Image generation creates new visual output from instructions
OCR is intended to create a new photograph
Visual analysis can describe content in an existing image

items.explanation: Generation creates new imagery, while OCR reads text from existing imagery and visual analysis interprets existing content. These are different workload directions.

references.title: Image inputs with Azure OpenAI models

items.prompt: A claims system needs policy number and incident date from standard forms. What should it use first?
items.answer: A suitable prebuilt or custom document analyzer
options: A suitable prebuilt or custom document analyzer
A clustering model with no schema
items.explanation: A document analyzer extracts defined fields from supplied forms. Key phrases, image creation, and unsupervised grouping do not produce the required grounded structure.

items.prompt: A custom field should contain one value from a controlled list of product categories. Which schema field behavior is appropriate?
items.explanation: A classification field constrains the result to an allowed category set. Image, speaker, and unconstrained numeric tasks do not meet that structured contract.

items.prompt: A recorded interview needs transcript, participants, and time-grounded topics. Which analyzer modality fits?
items.explanation: An audio analyzer can derive transcript and time-related insights from the recording. Image, speech output, and tabular prediction do not analyze the supplied audio.

items.prompt: An extraction result includes a value and its page region. What is the page region useful for?
items.answer: Showing the source evidence that supports the value
options: Showing the source evidence that supports the value
items.explanation: A grounded source region lets a user or workflow verify where the extracted value came from. It does not configure generation, authorization, or speech.

items.prompt: An analyzer works on sample PDFs but fails on mobile photographs. What should the next evaluation add?
items.answer: Representative photographed documents with realistic quality variation
options: Representative photographed documents with realistic quality variation
Only more copies of the original PDFs
Higher model temperature without new tests
items.explanation: Evaluation should include the actual input modality and quality variation expected in production. Duplicate clean PDFs, randomness, or unsafe credentials do not diagnose the gap.

items.prompt: A reusable definition of fields and processing behavior in Content Understanding is an {{blank}}.
items.explanation: An analyzer defines how Content Understanding processes content and which structured outputs it returns. Infrastructure zones, alert targets, and embeddings are different concepts.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about extraction confidence and review.

statements.text: A low-confidence consequential field can be routed to a person
A confidence score proves that a value is correct
Business thresholds should reflect the cost of extraction errors

items.explanation: Confidence is a workflow signal, not a guarantee. Validation thresholds and human review should reflect the consequences of incorrect extracted values.
